classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon VI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: If any one shall hold private assemblies outside of the Church, and, despising the canons, shall presume to perform ecclesiastical acts, the presbyter with the consent of the bishop refusing his permission, let him be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Whoso privately gathers a religious meeting let him be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Both these canons, [V. and VI.] forbid the existence of conventicles, and conventicle services.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: It already appears from the second article of the Synodal Letter of Gangra, that the Eustathians, through spiritual pride, separated themselves from the rest of the congregation, as being the pure and holy, avoided the public worship, and held private services of their own.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: The ninth, tenth, and eleventh articles of the Synodal Letter give us to understand that the Eustathians especially avoided the public services, when married clergy officiated.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: We might possibly conclude, from the words of the sixth canon: μὴ συνόντος τοῦ πρεσβυτέρου κατὰ γνώμην τοῦ ἐπισκόπου, that no priest performed any part in their private services; but it is more probable that the Eustathians, who did not reject the priesthood as such, but only abhorred the married clergy, had their own unmarried clergy, and that these officiated at their separate services.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: And the above-mentioned words of the canon do not the least contradict this supposition, for the very addition of the words κατὰ γνώμην τοῦ ἐπισκόπου indicate that the sectarian priests who performed the services of the Eustathians had received no permission to do so from the bishop of the place.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Thus did the Greek commentators, Balsamon, etc., and likewise Van Espen, interpret this canon.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: The meaning of this canon is very obscure.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: The Latin reads non conveniente presbytero, de episcopi sententia; and Lambert translates “without the presence of a priest, with consent of the bishop.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Hammond differs from this and renders thus, “without the concurrence of the presbyter and the consent of the bishop.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: I have translated literally and left the obscurity of the original.
